name: View Transitions API
performance_characteristics: Browser snapshots old and new states and animates them on the compositor; cheap relative to manual FLIP implementations
unsuitable_contexts: experiences that must work identically on older browsers without fallback
enterprise apps with strict legacy-browser support matrices
strengths: Native shared-element and cross-document/SPA page transitions with minimal code
Browser handles snapshotting and crossfade automatically via document.startViewTransition
Cross-document MPA transitions available via @view-transition CSS, no framework needed
Degrades gracefully: without support the state change just happens instantly
weaknesses: Newer baseline; cross-document support and Firefox lagged adoption, so progressive enhancement is required
Default crossfade can ignore prefers-reduced-motion unless authored carefully
Snapshot capture can be costly for very large or complex DOM trees
